hub_subtitle: Business English from the ground up -- your work, your team, your routine

lesson.menu_desc: Prepositions of time at / on / in, telling the time, and dates for work -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 11 -- Numbers, Dates & Schedules -- Times and Deadlines
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Danilo Febba | Aula 11 -- Numbers, Dates & Schedules

listenings.text: Hi Danilo, it's Rachel. Just a quick reminder about your schedule this week. The team meeting is on Monday at ten o'clock, not at nine. On Wednesday, we have the client call at half past two. And please remember: the project report deadline is on Friday, at noon. One more thing: your one-on-one with me is on Thursday, in the afternoon, at a quarter past four. Please be on time. See you on Monday. Bye!

I am a data engineering lead, and my week is very busy. I start work early, at eight o'clock in the morning. On Mondays, we have our team meeting at nine. I check the calendar every day, so I am never late for an appointment. We have many deadlines: most reports are due on Friday, at noon. In the afternoon, I answer emails and help my team. I finish at six o'clock in the evening. I like to be on time, so I always plan my day. A good calendar is the secret of a calm week.
